repo_notes.content: This repository is an experimental Recursive Self-Improvement / MetaForge system. Documentation should focus on executable self-improvement loops, validation gates, anti-cheat controls, lineage tracking, accepted/rejected modifications, benchmark logs, pytest outputs, and known failure modes.
Do not summarize this repository as a generic LLM-agent wrapper. The central claim is not prompt orchestration, but experimentally gated self-modification with evidence logs and reproducible tests.

Separate demonstrated behavior from speculative general-intelligence or unbounded capability claims. Emphasize what the code and logs actually show, where the evidence is weak, and which components are prototypes.
Prioritize human-authored documentation in docs/ as the canonical guide to the architecture, validation logic, evidence map, and limitations.

pages.title: System Overview
pages.purpose: Explain the overall RSI/MetaForge architecture, main files, execution flow, and how the components connect.

pages.title: Recursive Self-Improvement Loop
pages.purpose: Document where the system proposes, applies, validates, rejects, or accepts self-modifications.

pages.title: Validation Gates and Anti-Cheat Controls
pages.purpose: Explain mechanisms intended to prevent fake improvement, shallow mutation, benchmark gaming, or self-deception.

pages.title: Lineage Tracking and Evidence Logs
pages.purpose: Show how logs, lineage records, accepted patches, rejected patches, and test outputs support or weaken the self-improvement claim.

pages.title: Benchmark and Test Harness
pages.purpose: Document available tests, result files, battery outputs, pytest logs, demo modes, and how to reproduce reported behavior.

pages.title: Known Limitations and Failure Modes
pages.purpose: Clearly separate current experimental capability from unsupported broad self-improvement claims, including brittleness, current limits, and future work.
